title: cineraria pyrenaica
intro: Cineraria pyrenaica, also known as the Pyrenean cineraria, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family native to the Pyrenees mountains. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. Cineraria pyrenaica is often used in herbal preparations, including infusions, tinctures, and ointments, which are used to treat various health conditions, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a medical professional.
